questions.text: What is the opening command of Surah Al-A'la?
questions.answer: Exalt the name of your Lord, the Most High.

questions.text: What four actions of Allah are mentioned in Verses 2-5?
questions.answer: He created and proportioned, ordained and guided, and brought out the pasture.

questions.text: What happens to the pasture (vegetation) eventually?
questions.answer: Allah makes it 'blackened stubble' (Ghuthā'an ahwā).

questions.text: What promise does Allah give the Prophet (SAW) regarding the Quran?
questions.answer: We will make you recite, [O Muhammad], and you will not forget.

questions.text: Is there an exception to the Prophet not forgetting the Quran?
questions.answer: Yes, 'except what Allah wills' (Verse 7).

questions.text: Does Allah know what is declared and what is hidden?
questions.answer: Yes, 'He knows what is declared and what is hidden'.

questions.text: What will Allah ease for the Prophet (SAW)?
questions.answer: He will ease him toward the 'easy way' (Islam and its laws).

questions.text: When should the Prophet give a reminder?
questions.answer: If the reminder benefits (the people).

questions.text: Who will be reminded by the Prophet's message?
questions.answer: He who fears Allah.

questions.text: Who will avoid the reminder?
questions.answer: The 'most wretched' (Al-Ashqa).

questions.text: What is the fate of the wretched one?
questions.answer: He will enter the 'Great Fire' (An-Nar al-Kubra).

questions.text: Will the person in the Great Fire die or live?
questions.answer: He will neither die therein nor live (meaning a state of constant agony).

questions.text: Who is the one who has succeeded (Verse 14)?
questions.answer: He who has purified himself.

questions.text: What two things does the successful person do in Verse 15?
questions.answer: Remembers the name of his Lord and prays.

questions.text: What do people generally prefer according to Verse 16?
questions.answer: The life of this world.

questions.text: How is the Hereafter compared to the worldly life?
questions.answer: The Hereafter is better and more enduring (Khayrun wa Abqa).

questions.text: Were these teachings mentioned in previous scriptures?
questions.answer: Yes, 'Indeed, this is in the former scriptures' (Verse 18).

questions.text: Name the two Prophets whose scriptures are specifically mentioned at the end.
questions.answer: The scriptures of Ibrahim and Musa.

questions.text: What is the virtue of reciting this Surah in Friday and Eid prayers?
questions.answer: It was the Sunnah of the Prophet (SAW) to recite it frequently in these prayers.

questions.text: What is the meaning of 'Tazakka' in Verse 14?
questions.answer: Purifying oneself from shirk, sins, and bad character.
